exemplar.title: To what extent do pre-existing mental health conditions influence the prevalence of auto-immune arthritis in the US?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent do pre-existing mental health conditions influence the prevalence of auto-immune arthritis in the US?

strengths: Topic and research question are communicated clearly with defined variables and context
Interdisciplinary focus between biology and psychology is well established
Clear presentation of scope (U.S. adult population) through both title and introduction
weaknesses: Methodology justification is brief and lacks discussion of sampling criteria and biases
Ethical considerations and limitations of animal models are not addressed
Little explanation of how correlation versus causation will be distinguished
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two of the three strands (topic identification and research question) fully meet the topâband descriptors for Focus and Method, demonstrating clear and appropriate communication. The methodology strand is less well developed, falling short of the top band but still in the midârange. Overall, a score of 5 reflects strong focus and question formulation with only minor areas for methodological development.

strengths: Majority of sources are peer-reviewed journals and CDC datasets, demonstrating sound selection
Knowledge of biological mechanisms and psychological constructs is clear and integrated
Specialist terminology is generally accurate and used appropriately
weaknesses: Some popular-health sites and inconsistent hyphenation of terms reduce professional polish
Interdisciplinary integration is uneven, with explanatory passages outweighing analytical linkage
Acronyms like NSAIDs and DMARDs are not always spelled out at first mention
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The selection and use of source material are mostly relevant and appropriate, and knowledge is coherent with generally effective application of terminology. Minor lapses prevent an unqualified topâband placement, but overall performance merits a high midâband score of 5.

strengths: Research base is largely well chosen and directly relevant to the core question
Analysis goes beyond description to evaluate competing hypotheses and discuss uncertainty
Argument structure is logical, with clear progression and interdisciplinary connections
weaknesses: Depth of mechanistic analysis is sometimes limited, tending toward narrative rather than incisive critique
Statistical details (e.g., p-values, odds ratios) and external validity of key studies are not fully examined
Causal pathways behind geographic variation and broader environmental drivers remain under-explored
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands fall clearly within the upper half of the 4-6 band, with strong research relevance, coherent analysis, and a structured argument. While not quite at the ârigorous and consistently incisiveâ level required for the 10-12 band, the work is substantial and nuanced, warranting a score of 9 in Critical Thinking.

strengths: Report follows a clear academic structure with coherent headings and numbered sections
Visual elements are relevant and cited appropriately
Referencing and bibliography are present with correct in-text superscripts
weaknesses: Figure captions are minimal or mis-labelled and lack analytical integration
Citation and formatting styles are inconsistent (mix of MLA/APA conventions)
Table of contents has blank page numbers and some entries lack references
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation meets all academic conventions at a competent level: structured layout, relevant visuals, and a works-cited list. However, inconsistent formatting, incomplete captions, and navigation issues prevent a flawless top-band mark. A score of 4 reflects clear structure and presence of all required elements with room for improved consistency.

weaknesses: No RPPF or reflective evidence provided, making it impossible to assess engagement
No documented personal reflections on challenges or skill development
No indication of intellectual initiative or future research ideas
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: With no RPPF extracts or reflective material included, none of the Engagement strands can be evaluated, resulting in a score of 0.

strandSelections.reasoning: A logical progression (background â debate â conclusion) is evident, with headings aiding flow. Interdisciplinary links are present but sometimes superficial, and evaluation of evidence credibility is limited. Hence the argument is structured with âsome critical insightâ, meriting 4 in the 4-6 range.

A works-cited list is provided and in-text superscripts appear, yet formatting varies (MLA/APA hybrids, inconsistent italics, line breaks). Therefore referencing is present but not consistent, fitting the 1-2 band (score 2).

The topicâhow pre-existing mental-health conditions influence the prevalence of autoimmune arthritis in the U.S.âis explicitly stated in both the title block and the introduction. Scope, population (U.S. adults) and the interdisciplinary angle (biology + psychology) are clear, allowing the reader immediately to understand what the essay will cover. Minor phrasing issues (e.g., wording of prevalence/causality) do not obscure the focus, so the work meets the descriptor for clear and appropriate communication.

The report includes a table of contents, numbered sections and sub-headings, producing a recognizable academic layout. Minor inconsistencies (blank page numbers in the contents table, uneven figure numbering) reduce polish, so low-top-band (3) is justified.

The essay conveys solid biological knowledge (e.g., polymorphisms, HLA alleles, cytokine pathways) and links this to psychological constructs such as isolation and stress. However, large sections are explanatory rather than analytical and interdisciplinary integration is uneven. Knowledge is clear and generally well supported, fitting the 3-4 descriptor.
No RPPF extracts are supplied, so quality of reflections cannot be assessed, resulting in a band 0.

The essay moves beyond descriptionâe.g., contrasting causation hypotheses and discussing inflammatory markersâbut analysis often stops at correlation and lacks depth in evaluating competing explanations. Conclusions acknowledge uncertainty yet are somewhat generalized. This reflects coherent but not incisive analysis, consistent with a mid-band score of 4.

The research question is formally presented on the title page and repeated in the introduction. It specifies variables (pre-existing mental-health conditions, prevalence of autoimmune arthritis) and a clear geographical context (U.S.). Although ambitious, it is sufficiently delimited for a 4 000-word study and demonstrates a purposeful focus, satisfying the top-band descriptor.

Most cited studies and data sets directly address the mental-health/arthritis interface, and interview evidence strengthens relevance. A few general background sources persist, but overall the research base is appropriate and largely well matched to the question, aligning with the upper half of the 4-6 band.
The submission provides no documented examples of initiative (e.g., original data collection plans) within an RPPF, so intellectual initiative is unassessable and receives 0.

The student outlines use of scholarly articles, news pieces and expert interviews, but the rationale is brief and largely descriptive. There is little explanation of how sources were selected or how data would be triangulated; no explicit discussion of methodological limitations or ethics. Overall, methods are broadly appropriate yet only partially justified, fitting the 3-4 band.

A wide range of peer-reviewed journals (JCI, Nature Immunology, Rheumatology & Therapy), CDC datasets, and interview material is employed. While some popular-health sites appear, the majority of sources are scholarly and relevant. Selection is sound but not consistently âexpertly chosenâ, meriting the 3-4 band.
Without RPPF evidence, personal engagement cannot be evaluated; hence band 0 is awarded.

Three figures are inserted with citations, but captions are minimal and one figure reference is mis-labelled. Visuals are not always integrated into the text analytically. Thus visual presentation is partial, matching the 1-2 band (score 2).

Specialist terms (DMARDs, HPA axis, autoreactive T-cells) are used accurately and in context, showing familiarity with both biology and psychology vocabulary. Occasional minor lapses (âauto-immuneâ hyphenation, redundancy) prevent the work from showing the consistent depth demanded by the top band, so a secure 3-4 score is appropriate.

annotations.comment: Figure 2 is referred to in italics but lacks an explicit caption and figure number in the list; add a clear numbered caption.
Several figures on page 10 appear without captions or axis labels; include clear titles, units, and legend descriptions for each graphic.
Ensure each graph includes axis labels and units (e.g., % prevalence, age groups) so readers can interpret the data without ambiguity.
No RPPF or reflective comments are included, so engagement criteria cannot be assessed; include reflective planning and challenges in an RPPF section.
The research question is prominently stated and clearly defines variables and context, effectively focusing the investigation from the outset.
Footnote formatting is inconsistent, mixing MLA and APA conventions; adopt a uniform citation style throughout.
The methodology section briefly lists sources but lacks discussion of sampling criteria, ethical considerations, and potential biases.
Much of the biological description is solid, but the text often remains narrative rather than critically evaluating competing theories.
The visual effectively conveys mechanistic detail but is not analyzed in-text; integrate interpretation of key features into the discussion.
Animal model methods are mentioned but not justified in relation to human populations; clarify sampling and translational limitations.
Including the word count at the start meets IB formatting requirements and demonstrates attention to formal submission guidelines.
The theme and subjects are clearly defined, establishing a strong interdisciplinary foundation between biology and psychology.
Terminology like âauto-immuneâ is hyphenated inconsistently throughout; standardize spelling (autoimmune) to maintain professional consistency.
Several entries in the table of contents lack page numbers, which makes navigation difficult; ensure each heading has a corresponding page reference.
The table of contents uses literal HTML tags (e.g., â<table>â) and uneven formatting, indicating a formatting glitch that should be cleaned.
The introduction outlines background well but does not yet clarify how correlation versus causation will be distinguished; consider adding explicit definitions and scope.
The inline fraction â\frac{2}{3}â appears as raw MathML rather than formatted LaTeX; ensure all equations follow the $$...$$ convention.
Figure 1 is relevant but the caption only cites the source; include a brief description of what the figure illustrates to guide the reader.
The discussion of HLA allele associations would benefit from quantitative measures (e.g., odds ratios) to strengthen the genetic argument.
The large cohort study is cited but lacks discussion of statistical thresholds (e.g., p-values); include this to substantiate claims of significance.
The comorbidity section outlines various conditions but does not explicitly link back to the core research question; strengthen connections.
Expert interview excerpts provide valuable insight into inflammatory mechanisms and psychosocial factors.
Maintain consistent terminology by choosing either âautoimmuneâ or âauto-immuneâ and applying it throughout the report.
The role of HPA axis dysregulation is noted but not fully dissected; add deeper mechanistic analysis linking stress hormones to cytokine activity.
The state-by-state CDC statistics are well integrated and enrich the epidemiological context of the argument.
Although descriptive epidemiology is strong, the causal pathways behind geographic variation remain unexplored; include analysis of potential social or environmental drivers.
Acronyms such as NSAIDs and DMARDs should be spelled out at first mention (e.g., Nonsteroidal Anti-Inflammatory Drugs) to aid reader comprehension.
The debate sections are structured logically, but the Land et al. studyâs methodological limits are not critiqued; add evaluation of its external validity.
The works-cited list exhibits inconsistent formatting and incomplete entries; standardize according to a single citation style and complete missing details.
The review of methodology is concise but lacks discussion of data triangulation and ethical considerations; expand to justify your approachâs rigour.
The title placement, candidate details, and word count all adhere to formatting guidelines, demonstrating careful attention to presentation.
